LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHANDRA CAROL'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. THE POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS REQUIRES GRANTING THIS MOTION, ESPECIALLY SINCE THE MOVING PARTY WAS SELF-REPRESENTED AT THE TIME OF THE DEFAULT, THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION IS PENDING AND THERE IS NO PREJUDICE TO THE PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CHANDRA CAROL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/MM

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-21-2011 Continued To Dec-16-11 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Oct-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lucia MacDonald, Esq., from Bingham McCutchen LLP, 415-393-2630, for Defendant and moving party

No other appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT CHANDRA CAROL'S Motion To Set Aside Default is granted. The policy of deciding cases on their merits requires granting this motion, especially since the moving party was self-represented at the time of the default, the unlawful detainer action is pending and there is no prejudice to the plaintiff. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
